title: ctenitis decaryana
intro: Ctenitis decaryana, also known as the Hook fern, is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. The plant is native to New Zealand and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Ctenitis decaryana include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and protecting against oxidative stress, making it a valuable plant for treating various health conditions. Herbal preparations of Ctenitis decaryana include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
